lib/components/op_properties/ChamferSect: Cut the chamfer twice — once at the rough feed (cleanup) and once at the tool's finish-set feed for surface quality.
lib/components/op_properties/CycleMarker: The pipeline emits one comment line:
Wrapped with `--- … ---` and emitted as a G-code comment at this slot. No controller motion.
lib/components/op_properties/DrillSectio: G81 — single plunge to depth, retract.
G83 — peck with full retract to clearance plane between pecks.

Spot pre-pass: before the main drill, the machine spots each hole with a shallow centre mark using a stiffer tool, so a twist drill doesn't walk on hard or polished stock. Drill, then T<n> M6 to the spot tool, spot every hole, then back to the drill.
lib/components/op_properties/GcodeInclud: G-code text — edit by hand or pick a file above.
The G-code that ships in the program. Edit by hand if you need to tweak after loading. Variable tokens are substituted at Generate time.
The pipeline emits the content verbatim at this slot after substituting these tokens:
When on, each unsimulated line fires its own warning with the line offset and reason — useful for debugging an exotic block. When off (default), only the counted summary fires.
lib/components/op_properties/HomingSecti: Retract to safe Z after G28

When checked, the pipeline follows the G28 with a rapid G0 Z<safe> at the op's fastMoveZ so the next op starts from a known clearance. Most controllers leave the spindle wherever machine zero puts it, so the default is on.
lib/components/op_properties/PatternSect: Angle of the first instance — shifts the whole ring so it doesn't have to start at 0°.
Pattern shape — Linear array, rectangular Grid, or Polar (rotational) array.

Run this operation once per pattern instance with the source geometry translated or rotated. The original geometry stays at the (0, 0) / 0° instance — single-count patterns are equivalent to no pattern.
Total instances around the center, including the original at 0°.
lib/components/op_properties/PocketSecti: Convert this Pocket into a Pocket Outside operation: the pipeline auto-derives a frame around the selection at generate time and carves the area BETWEEN the frame and the selection.
Convert this op to a Pocket Outside by attaching a synthetic frame around its source selection.
Corner radius (mm) for the rounded rectangle. Empty = same as padding.
Halfpipe walks the slot's medial axis at varying Z so the cut floor matches the chosen profile. Tool kind: ball-nose for circular_arc, V-bit for v_bottom.
Pipe radius in mm. Match this to the ball-nose cutter's radius for a true half-pipe.
Shape of the synthetic frame the pipeline derives from your selection at generate time.
Trochoidal will override plunge to Helix.
V-bit included angle in degrees. Same semantics as the V-Carve tip angle.
lib/components/op_properties/ProbeSectio: . The controller halts at the trigger.
lib/components/op_properties/ProfileSect: Lead-in style. Off: rapid + plunge directly to the contour start. Straight: rapid to a point perpendicular to the start, then linear into the contour. Arc: tangent quarter-arc roll-on so the cutter eases into the cut without dwelling at the start.
lib/components/op_properties/RasterEngra: Laser S at black (darkest pixels) — the hottest burn.
Laser S emitted for an 'on' (burning) pixel.
Live preview of the engrave: darker = hotter burn.
Ordered-dither tile size. Larger = finer tonal steps, coarser tile.
Overscan past each row edge as a fraction of the row length, so the head reaches commanded power before crossing pixels. 0 = none.
Physical width of the engrave on the workpiece. Height follows the image aspect ratio.
Raster engraving burns a grayscale image row-by-row — dark pixels burn hotter. Tune the power curve so the preview matches the contrast you want before kicking off a long run.
lib/components/op_properties/ReliefMillS: Allowed ridge height left between adjacent passes. Smaller = finer finish, longer cut.
Physical width of the relief on the workpiece. Height follows the image aspect ratio.
Point spacing along each scanline. Finer = smoother path, more G-code.
Relief surfacing is a finish pass — rough the bulk first with a flat endmill (Pocket / Profile), then this ball-nose pass cleans the surface.
Swap the mapping so dark areas become high and light areas become deep.
lib/components/op_properties/TabsSection: Click on a closed contour in the 2D canvas to place a tab. Click on an existing tab to remove it.

How tab positions are sourced for this op. Off ignores tabs entirely. Auto evenly spaces N tabs on each closed contour. Manual lets you click on the 2D canvas to place individual tabs. Mixed combines both.
Number of tabs to auto-place evenly around each closed contour.
Width of each bridge along the cut path. Default 10 mm.
Z clearance the cutter lifts to over each tab. Default 1 mm.
lib/components/op_properties/ThreadSecti: Thread requires a closed circle as the source.
Z descent per full revolution. Picks the thread series: M6×1.0 → 1.0 mm, M3×0.5 → 0.5 mm. Positive value.
lib/components/op_properties/VCarveSecti: Planned for a future release: re-cut only the points whose first pass fell short of the geometric target depth. The control is disabled until the refinement pass ships.
